name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
overview: RAG retrieves relevant information from a local document index to augment LLM prompt contexts for factual answering.

steps.name: Ingestion & Chunking
steps.what: Parse documents and split text into smaller chunks.
steps.decision: Use chunk size 512 with 10% overlap as baseline.

steps.name: Embedding
steps.what: Encode each text chunk into a dense vector using a sentence embedding model.
steps.decision: Choose BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 for high-accuracy multilingual retrieval; for latency-sensitive systems, downsize to gte-large or enable ONNX quantization.

steps.name: Vector Storage
steps.what: Store chunk embeddings in a vector database for efficient approximate nearest neighbor search.
steps.decision: FAISS for single-machine in-memory search; ChromaDB for persistent local storage; Qdrant for multi-tenant cloud deployments with built-in filtering.

steps.name: Query Processing
steps.what: Embed the user's query using the same embedding model and retrieve top-k similar chunks.
steps.decision: Start with top-k=5; increase to 10-20 for long-form queries. Always normalize query embeddings before search if the embedding model uses cosine similarity.

steps.name: Reranking
steps.what: Score the retrieved chunks against the query with a cross-encoder to improve relevance ordering.
steps.decision: Add a reranker when retrieval precision drops below 70% on held-out queries. Keep reranker top-n at 3-5 to avoid exceeding the LLM context window.

steps.name: Generation
steps.what: Inject the retrieved context into a prompt template and call the LLM to generate a grounded answer.
steps.decision: Use a strict prompt template that separates context from the question. Limit context to 3000 tokens to leave room for the answer and system prompt.

common_failure_points: Chunk boundaries splitting sentences across semantic units, causing retrieval to miss half of a critical fact.
Using a different embedding model at query time than at indexing time, producing incompatible vector spaces and zero relevant hits.
Retrieving outdated document versions because the vector store lacks a versioning or timestamp filter.
Hallucination when the LLM ignores the provided context and falls back on parametric knowledge due to a weakly structured prompt.
Context window overflow because the combined prompt and retrieved chunks exceed the LLM's maximum token limit, causing silent truncation of the system prompt.
